headlines.title: Can TXN's Embedded Business Extend Its Double-Digit Growth Run?
headlines.summary: Texas Instruments' embedded processing unit posts 12% revenue growth as industrial demand improves, but can the rebound hold amid uneven markets?

headlines.title: Microchip Technology (MCHP) Joins Russell Growth Indexes, Is It Still 22% Undervalued?
headlines.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) is back in focus after a series of index changes and a move to make key MPLAB XC Pro compilers and its machine learning development suite available to developers at no cost. See our latest analysis for Microchip Technology. The index reshuffle into several Russell growth benchmarks and the free access to MPLAB tools has coincided with a 23.34% 90 day share price return and a 35.72% year to date share price return. The 1 year total shareholder return is 20.

headlines.title: Microchip (MCHP) Stock May Be 26% Above Fair Value As Recovery Guidance Lands
headlines.summary: Microchip Technology stock has delivered a 32.5% gain over the past five years, yet the current valuation picture is pulled in two directions, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate pointing to a premium while market based multiples still screen as supportive. A 32.5% total return over five years suggests Microchip Technology has rewarded patient shareholders and raises the question of how much of that performance is now reflected in the price.

headlines.title: Microchip (MCHP) Makes Its MPLAB Pro Compilers And ML Suite Free
headlines.summary: Microchip Technology (NasdaqGS:MCHP) has made its MPLAB XC Pro Compilers available free of charge. The company has also removed licensing costs for its MPLAB Machine Learning Development Suite. This change opens access to advanced optimization and embedded ML tools for a wider pool of developers. Microchip Technology, a major supplier of microcontrollers and related development tools, sits at the intersection of embedded computing and connected devices.

headlines.title: Microchip Technology (MCHP) Stock Sees Fair Value Lift As Analysts Raise Targets
headlines.summary: Analysts have lifted their modelled fair value for Microchip Technology from US$86.67 to US$112.96, resetting the price anchor they use to frame the stock. The move comes as research teams weigh the company’s latest disclosures, including the new data center revenue breakout, recent pricing actions, and a wave of higher targets that mix optimism with caution.

description: Microchip Technology Incorporated develops, manufactures, and sells smart, connected, and secure embedded control solutions in the Americas, Europe, and Asia. It operates in two segments, Semiconductor Products and Technology Licensing. The company offers general-purpose 8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit mixed-signal microcontrollers; 32-bit and 64-bit embedded mixed-signal microprocessors; and specialized mixed-signal microcontrollers for automotive, industrial, computing, communications, lighting, power supplies, motor control, human-machine interface, security, wired connectivity, and wireless connectivity applications. It also provides analog products, including power management, linear, mixed-signal, high voltage, thermal management, discrete diodes and MOSFETs, radio frequency (RF), gate drivers, safety, security, timing, application-specific standard products, USB, Ethernet, wireless, and other interface products; field-programmable gate array (FPGA) products; and application development tools that enable system designers to program mixed-signal microcontroller, FPGA, and microprocessor products. In addition, the company offers memory products consisting of serial electrically erasable programmable read-only memory, serial flash memories, parallel flash memories, serial static random-access memory, and electrically erasable random-access memory for footprint devices. Further, it licenses its SuperFlash embedded flash and non-volatile memory technologies to foundries, integrated device manufacturers, and design partners for use in the manufacture of microcontroller products, gate arrays, RF, analog, and neuromorphic compute products. Additionally, the company offers engineering services; wafer foundry, assembly, and test subcontracting manufacturing services; and timing systems, application-specific integrated circuits, and products for aerospace applications. The company was incorporated in 1989 and is headquartered in Chandler, Arizona.
